findings.title: Weak hash algorithm (MD5/SHA1)
findings.description: MD5 and SHA1 are cryptographically broken.
findings.suggested_fix: Use SHA-256 or better (SHA-3, BLAKE3).
findings.why_it_matters: Weak hashes can be cracked or collided.

findings.suggested_fix: Use SHA-256 or better (SHA-3, BLAKE3).
findings.why_it_matters: Weak hashes can be cracked or collided.

findings.title: Unreachable code after return
findings.description:
Code after return/throw/exit will never execute:
```
#[cfg(debug_assertions)]
```
findings.suggested_fix: Remove unreachable code or fix control flow logic.
findings.why_it_matters: Unreachable code indicates logic errors and adds confusion.

findings.title: Panic risk: unwrap() without context
findings.description: Using `unwrap()` can cause panics. Consider `?`, `unwrap_or`, or proper error handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/axum/axum/src/response/redirect.rs::impl<Redirect>::location:66` separates components of [1524, 10]
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/axum/axum/src/response/redirect.rs::impl<Redirect>::location:66` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [1524, 10]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Panic risk: unwrap() without context
findings.description: Using `unwrap()` can cause panics. Consider `?`, `unwrap_or`, or proper error handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: High panic density in `strip_prefix`: 7 panic-family calls
findings.description: Function `strip_prefix` contains 7 calls to `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, or `panic!()` at line 65, line 72, line 112, line 113, line 114, line 115, line 121. This makes the function fragile and likely to panic at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix:
Reduce panic points by:
- Using `?` operator for error propagation
- Using `unwrap_or` / `unwrap_or_default` / `unwrap_or_else`
- Returning `Result<T, E>` instead of panicking
- Extracting fallible logic into a helper that returns Result
findings.why_it_matters: Functions with many panic points are fragile. A single unexpected None/Err will crash the program. Consolidating error handling makes code more robust and easier to reason about.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/axum/axum/src/serve/listener.rs::impl<Listener for TapIo<L, F>>::accept:234` separates components of [1520, 14]
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/axum/axum/src/serve/listener.rs::impl<Listener for TapIo<L, F>>::accept:234` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [1520, 14]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Mutex poisoning risk
findings.description: Using .lock().unwrap() will panic if the mutex is poisoned. Consider handling the PoisonError or using parking_lot::Mutex.
findings.suggested_fix:
Handle poisoning: `mutex.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`
Or use `parking_lot::Mutex` which has no poisoning.
findings.why_it_matters: Poisoned mutexes cause cascading panics across threads.

findings.title: Long method: extract_fields (227 lines)
findings.description: Function 'extract_fields' has 227 lines (effective threshold: 150).
findings.suggested_fix: Break into smaller, focused functions.
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: High file-level panic density: 32 panic-family calls
findings.description: File `examples/compression/src/tests.rs` contains 32 total panic-family calls (`.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, `panic!()`) outside of test code. Consider refactoring to reduce panic surface.
findings.suggested_fix:
Consider:
- Introducing a module-level error type
- Replacing unwrap chains with `?` propagation
- Using `anyhow::Context` for better error messages
findings.why_it_matters: Files with many panic points are hard to maintain and make the overall application fragile. Centralizing error handling improves reliability.
